text:
There once was a wise old tortoise named Tortoise. He lived alone in the quiet woods, away from the hustle and bustle of the world. It was his favorite place because there were no other humans, no other creatures. He felt entirely at home among the leaves and underbrush.

One day, as Tortoise went his slow, steady way through the forest, he stumbled upon a glissando of tiny bells. Something brightened his spirit and he followed the sound. He found a garden where bright butterflies flitted about, laughing and playing. They saw the wise old tortoise approaching and gathered around, giggling and fluttering closer.

"Hello, kind old sir!" one of the butterflies exclaimed. "We've never seen anyone quite like you! What brings you to our beautiful garden?"

"I'm not quite sure what I'm doing here," Tortoise replied. "But I enjoy these quiet moments. This garden suits my calmness."
